v4:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds metadata (pom descriptions and quarkus-extension.yaml files) to the SSE and stdio transport extensions so they render properly in code.quarkus and Maven Central. The changes follow repo conventions for keywords/categories/status, but contain a typo and a likely missing field.

POINTS:
- [transports/stdio/pom.xml:14] Typo in description: `"stanard input/output"` should be `"standard input/output"`.
- [transports/sse/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml] Missing `description:` field. The core extension at `core/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml` includes a top-level `description` key (e.g. `"This extension enables developers to implement the MCP server features easily."`). Without this, the Quarkus extension catalog will have no description to display for the SSE transport. Add a `description:` line matching the pom.xml description (or similar).
- [transports/stdio/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml] Same as above — missing `description:` field. Add a top-level `description` key for consistency with the core extension's YAML.
- [transports/sse/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml, transports/stdio/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml] The `guide` URL is set to `https://docs.quarkiverse.io/quarkus-mcp-server/dev/index.html`, whereas the core extension's guide link is commented out with a different path. Confirm that this guide URL is correct and the docs exist; if not yet published, consider commenting it out like the core extension does to avoid broken links.
v5:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds extension metadata (pom.xml descriptions and quarkus-extension.yaml files) to the SSE and stdio transport modules so they display correctly in code.quarkus and Maven Central. The structure aligns with project conventions, but there is a typo in one description and a potential missing field worth clarifying.

POINTS:
- [transports/stdio/pom.xml:14] Typo in description: "stanard" should be "standard". The text reads "...using stanard input/output (stdio)." → fix to "standard".
- [transports/sse/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml, transports/stdio/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml] Both new files omit a top-level `description` field. The existing `core/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml` includes one (`description: This extension enables developers to implement the MCP server features easily.`). Is this intentional for transport extensions, or should brief descriptions be added here as well for consistency?
- Minor note (not blocking): The guide URL is active in the new files but commented out in core/runtime's quarkus-extension.yaml. If the docs at `https://docs.quarkiverse.io/quarkus-mcp-server/dev/index.html` are live, this is correct and could even be uncommented in core. If not yet published, consider commenting it out to match core's approach.
